en: Hello, Olivia. Long time, no see. How have you been?
Hey, Jake. Yes, it's been ages, hasn't it?
I've been really good. My job keeps me really busy. How about you?
That's great to hear. Same here. I haven't had much time to myself. I'm taking a couple of online courses.
Oh, how cool is that? What kind of courses?
I'm taking a course on digital marketing, and another on graphic design. They are really interesting. Are you still working at the same place?
Yes, nothing new there. I got a promotion a while back, so I have a lot more responsibility.
That's great news. Congratulations, Olivia.
Hi, Adam. I haven't seen you around recently. What's new?
Hi, Sandy. Oh, not much, really. The kids have been keeping me busy.
That's great. You have three kids, right?
You got it. Three little rugrats. They are a real handful, too.
Haha, I bet. So, do you have any plans for the long weekend?
Nothing special, really. I'm thinking about taking the kids to the zoo. But I'm not a big fan of crowded places and waiting in long lines.
How about you? Do you have any plans?
I hear you there. I can't stand big crowds, either. I'm planning to just take it easy this weekend. I'm going to catch up on some housework, and most of all, catch up on some sleep.
That sounds like a great plan.
Hey, Melinda. Wow, you look like you just won the lottery. You have a smile from ear to ear.
Hi, Larry. Yes. Actually, I am having a great day today.
I just received the results of my final exam and I passed. I'm over the moon, really.
Congratulations. That's wonderful news. So, what's next?
Good question. I'm going to do an internship with a company that's based in New York.
What? Are you kidding me? So, you're quitting?
Yes. I just handed in my resignation. My last day is on the 30th.
Oh, what a bummer. You'll really be missed around here.
That's really nice of you to say, Larry. I'll sure miss everyone, too.
Hi, Jane. What's up?
Oh, not much. I'm feeling a bit burned out. I'm looking forward to the weekend.
Well, that's not good. Why are you feeling burned out?
I've been working a lot of overtime recently. I have a lot on my plate and I don't feel like I'm making a dent in it.
That isn't good to hear, Jane. Is there anything that I could do to help?
I don't think so. Thanks for asking, though, Frank.
Well, maybe I can take some things off your plate. Why don't I give you a hand with some of the reports?
Well, to be honest, I could use the help. Would you mind?
Hi, Jimmy. How's it going?
I couldn't be better, actually. What a great day it's been so far.
Oh, that's good to hear. What's up?
I just found out that I'm in the running for a promotion. It's between me and another guy.
Oh, how exciting! When will you find out?
Well, I have a second interview for the position next Wednesday. I'm a little nervous. I've got the butterflies just thinking about it.
You'll do great, I'm sure. Is there anything that you can do to prepare?
Yeah. I need to brush up on my French. The position is based here. But if I get the job, I'll have to travel to France a lot.
How cool! I didn't know that you could speak French.
Hello, Amanda. How are things with you?
Oh, hi, Jeff. I could be better, actually. My dog died yesterday.
Oh, I'm so sorry to hear that. I know how much pets are a part of the family. I'm a dog person myself.
Oh, I'm really so sorry for your loss, Amanda.
Thanks, Jeff. Yeah, he was such a good boy. He was 16, so he lived a pretty good life, I guess.
What kind of dog was he?
He was a black lab. We rescued him from the local shelter. It's going to feel really strange walking into my house and not being greeted by him every day.
What a bummer! Why don't you take the rest of the day off, Amanda?
I mean, I don't think you'll be able to concentrate. And a day off might do you a world of good.
Thanks, Jeff. I think I'll try to tough it out. But maybe I'll leave a little bit early.
I'll see you soon.
